vision: Deliver an engaging dragon-themed video game that fulfills players' fantasy of interacting with mythical creatures through accessible and entertaining gameplay.

epics.name: Core Dragon Interaction
epics.description: Foundational system enabling players to see and interact with dragons in the game world

stories.title: View dragon on screen
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to see a dragon displayed on screen, so that I can begin engaging with the dragon-themed content
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is launched When the main game screen loads Then at least one dragon is visibly rendered on screen
Given a dragon is displayed When the player views the screen Then the dragon has idle animation indicating it is alive
stories.notes: Walking skeleton - establishes core visual element

stories.title: Basic dragon responds to player input
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want the dragon to respond when I interact with it, so that I feel a sense of connection with the creature
acceptance_criteria: Given a dragon is on screen When the player clicks/taps on the dragon Then the dragon performs a response animation
Given a dragon is on screen When the player interacts with the dragon Then audio feedback accompanies the response

stories.title: Dragon displays multiple emotional states
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want the dragon to show different emotions, so that it feels like a living creature with personality
acceptance_criteria: Given a dragon exists When game conditions change Then the dragon displays at least 3 distinct emotional states
Given the dragon's emotional state changes When the player observes Then visual indicators clearly communicate the current state
stories.notes: Depends on core interaction being established

epics.name: Game Application Foundation
epics.description: Technical infrastructure to launch, run, and exit the game application

stories.title: Launch game application successfully
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to launch the game application, so that I can start playing
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is installed When the player initiates launch Then the application opens within 10 seconds
Given the application is launching When loading completes Then a start screen or main game view is displayed

stories.title: Exit game cleanly
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to exit the game at any time, so that I can stop playing when I choose
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is running When the player triggers exit Then the application closes without errors
Given the player exits the game When they relaunch later Then no corruption or crash occurs

stories.title: Pause and resume gameplay
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to pause the game, so that I can take breaks without losing my place
acceptance_criteria: Given gameplay is active When the player triggers pause Then all game actions halt immediately
Given the game is paused When the player resumes Then gameplay continues from the exact paused state

epics.name: Core Gameplay Loop
epics.description: Primary game mechanics that define what players do and how they progress

stories.title: Perform primary gameplay action
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to perform a core gameplay action, so that I have something meaningful to do in the game
acceptance_criteria: Given the player is in the game When they perform the primary action Then visible feedback confirms the action occurred
Given an action is performed When successful Then the game state changes in a perceivable way
stories.notes: CRITICAL GAP: Core mechanic undefined - placeholder for primary gameplay. Requires clarification.

stories.title: Understand current objective
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to understand what I should be doing, so that I don't feel lost or confused
acceptance_criteria: Given gameplay has started When the player looks at the screen Then a current goal or objective is visible or implied
Given an objective exists When the player completes it Then feedback indicates completion
stories.notes: CRITICAL GAP: Win/lose conditions undefined - requires clarification

stories.title: Experience consequence of actions
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want my actions to have consequences, so that my choices feel meaningful
acceptance_criteria: Given the player performs actions When actions accumulate Then a measurable outcome results
Given positive actions When evaluated Then the player receives positive reinforcement
stories.notes: Adds depth to undefined core loop

epics.name: Visual Presentation
epics.description: Art, graphics, and visual elements that create the dragon fantasy aesthetic

stories.title: View dragon with appealing visuals
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want the dragon to look visually appealing, so that the fantasy feels immersive
acceptance_criteria: Given a dragon is rendered When the player views it Then the dragon has complete visual assets with no placeholder graphics
Given the dragon is displayed When observed Then it matches an intentional art style consistently
stories.notes: CRITICAL GAP: Art style undefined - requires clarification on 2D/3D/realistic/cartoon

stories.title: View game environment background
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want to see an environment around the dragon, so that the world feels complete
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is running When the player views the screen Then a background environment is visible behind the dragon
Given an environment exists When observed Then it complements the dragon aesthetic

stories.title: View visual effects for interactions
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to see visual effects when things happen, so that the game feels responsive and exciting
acceptance_criteria: Given the player interacts with elements When interaction occurs Then a visual effect accompanies the action
Given visual effects play When observed Then they do not obscure critical gameplay information

epics.name: Audio Experience
epics.description: Sound effects and music that enhance the dragon fantasy atmosphere

stories.title: Hear dragon sounds
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want to hear dragon sounds, so that the creature feels real and powerful
acceptance_criteria: Given a dragon is on screen When the dragon performs actions Then appropriate dragon sounds play
Given dragon sounds exist When played Then audio quality is clear without distortion
stories.notes: Key immersion element for fantasy enthusiasts

stories.title: Hear background music
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to hear background music, so that the game has atmosphere
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is running When gameplay is active Then background music plays
Given music is playing When the player listens Then it loops seamlessly without jarring transitions

stories.title: Control audio volume
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to control the volume, so that I can play in different environments
acceptance_criteria: Given audio is playing When the player accesses settings Then volume controls are available
Given volume is adjusted When the player changes it Then audio levels update immediately

epics.name: Player Progress
epics.description: Systems that track and save player advancement and achievements

stories.title: Save game progress automatically
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want my progress saved automatically, so that I don't lose my achievements
acceptance_criteria: Given the player has made progress When a save trigger occurs Then progress is persisted locally
Given progress was saved When the player returns later Then previous state is restored
stories.notes: Essential for retention - exact save triggers depend on core loop

stories.title: View progress indicator
stories.user_story: As a Fantasy Enthusiast, I want to see my progress, so that I feel a sense of accomplishment
acceptance_criteria: Given the player has made progress When they check status Then a progress indicator shows current standing
Given progress is displayed When viewed Then it accurately reflects saved state

epics.name: User Interface
epics.description: Menus, controls, and interface elements for navigating the game

stories.title: Navigate main menu
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want a main menu, so that I can access different game options
acceptance_criteria: Given the game launches When the start screen appears Then a main menu with options is displayed
Given the main menu is shown When the player selects an option Then the corresponding screen or action loads

stories.title: Understand controls intuitively
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want controls to be intuitive, so that I can play without reading instructions
acceptance_criteria: Given gameplay begins When the player attempts interaction Then standard platform controls work as expected
Given controls exist When first used Then visual or contextual hints guide the player
stories.notes: CRITICAL GAP: Platform undefined affects control scheme

stories.title: Access help or tutorial
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to access help if needed, so that I can learn mechanics I don't understand
acceptance_criteria: Given the player needs guidance When they access help Then instructions for core mechanics are displayed
Given help is accessed When the player reads it Then they can return to gameplay easily

epics.name: Platform Deployment
epics.description: Technical requirements for deploying the game to target platform(s)

stories.title: Run game on target platform
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want to play the game on my device, so that I can access the experience
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is built When deployed to target platform Then it installs and launches successfully
Given the game is running When played Then performance is acceptable without major lag or crashes
stories.notes: CRITICAL GAP: Platform completely undefined - Mobile/PC/Console/Web requires clarification

stories.title: Meet platform technical requirements
stories.user_story: As a Casual Gamer, I want the game to run smoothly, so that my experience is not frustrating
acceptance_criteria: Given the game is running When performance is measured Then frame rate meets platform standards
Given the game is installed When storage is checked Then install size is reasonable for platform
